Add Crystal Mine rhythm mining game to BlockVerse

Implement the full Crystal Mine game with:
- 3-column mine shaft with 8 ore types (Coal through Diamond)
- Beat-synced mining using audioContext.currentTime for precision
- BPM progression from 100 to 180 BPM across depth layers
- Timing windows narrowing from ±150ms to ±65ms with depth
- C major pentatonic ore tones creating emergent melody
- 5-miss collapse mechanic with screen shake and particles
- Streak counter, depth tracking, personal best via localStorage
- Results screen with ore breakdown and next-ore tease
- All audio synthesized via Web Audio API (shared AudioContext)
- Mobile-optimized with touchstart events and 80px touch targets

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: switch to Gemini 2.5 Flash-Lite primary, reduce quota to 10/month

- Primary LLM: Gemini 2.5 Flash-Lite (65K output, no time limit)
- Fallback: CF Workers AI GLM-4.7-Flash
- Monthly generation quota: 50 → 10 per user (warning at 7)

CF Workers AI has ~30s inference timeout that truncates long scene
generation. Gemini Flash-Lite has no such limit and better Korean
quality at comparable cost ($0.10/$0.40 per M tokens).

feat: wire NextRead view into App

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix badge celebration popups never appearing

Three critical bugs prevented the full-screen badge celebration modal
from ever showing:

1. BadgeContext readyRef gate silently discarded badges that arrived
   during the first ~2 seconds after login, marking them as "seen"
   permanently. Replaced with a pending queue that processes after init.

2. useBadgeChecker only showed a toast for earned badges — never called
   celebrateBadge() to trigger the modal. Now fetches full badge data
   and queues the celebration overlay.

3. Notification race condition: context checked if a notification existed
   before showing the popup, but the DB trigger hadn't fired yet. Removed
   this dependency — show the modal immediately on badge award.

Also added error logging to all previously silent catch blocks.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
feat: parseHuffmanTreeDescriptor FSE-path + parseHuffmanWeightsFse completeness (#1130)

* feat: parseHuffmanTreeDescriptor FSE-path + parseHuffmanWeightsFse completeness (#1128)

Add two completeness theorems for the FSE-coded Huffman weight path:

- `parseHuffmanWeightsFse_succeeds`: when data has enough bytes and all
  sub-calls (decodeFseDistribution, buildFseTable, BackwardBitReader.init,
  decodeFseSymbolsAll) succeed, parseHuffmanWeightsFse succeeds
- `parseHuffmanTreeDescriptor_succeeds_fse`: when headerByte ∈ 1..127,
  parseHuffmanWeightsFse succeeds, and buildZstdHuffmanTable succeeds,
  parseHuffmanTreeDescriptor succeeds

This completes parseHuffmanTreeDescriptor coverage for all paths:
- Direct (headerByte ≥ 128): merged in #1118
- FSE (headerByte ∈ 1..127): this commit
- Error (headerByte = 0): always fails, no theorem needed

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* doc: progress entry for parseHuffmanTreeDescriptor FSE-path completeness

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

feat: expandable peer rows with per-link interface/routing-table, relay taint guard, a11y fixes

Add ifName and routingTable to connection reports (agent → server → frontend).
Peer rows in dashboard are now clickable — expanding to show that peer's
connections with interface name, routing table, mode, endpoint, and stats.

Monitor now tracks relay-forwarded packets to avoid false direct-alive signals
when rx-bytes increase is caused by relay proxy feeding packets into AWG.

Accessibility: keyboard support on peer rows and sort headers, aria-expanded,
aria-sort, role=alert on errors, increased touch targets, table-compact for
nested tables, consistent probing badge titles.

commit.message:
Track E: BackwardBitReader.init completeness (#1131)

* feat: add lean-parsing-completeness skill

New skill documenting the proof pattern for parsing completeness theorems —
showing that well-formed input implies native parser success. Covers the
core technique (unfold, guard discharge, case-split intermediates, extract
equalities), common sub-patterns (PUnit artifacts, getElem! bounds, BEq
bridging, sizeFormat dispatch), eliminator lemma factoring, and anti-patterns.

Distilled from 8+ parsing completeness theorems across DEFLATE and Zstd.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* doc: add congrArg and letFun patterns to lean-dependent-types skill

Two patterns that recurred across multiple sessions but weren't documented:

1. congrArg for large list/array rewrites: when rw hits maxRecDepth on
   288-element lists, use congrArg to lift equality through a function
   wrapper instead.

2. letFun for have bindings: when unfold leaves have bindings in the
   elaborated term, use simp only [letFun] to reduce them. The linter
   false positive on this is noted.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* doc: add join point and List.replicate patterns to lean-monad-proofs skill

Three additions based on recurring struggles across multiple sessions:

1. Join points (__do_jp): documents how early return in do-blocks creates
   join points that cause unfold mismatches, with the rewrite to avoid them.

2. List.replicate expansion: documents how unfold expands replicate into
   large literal lists, with the definitional-alias workaround.

3. Cross-reference to new lean-parsing-completeness skill.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* doc: progress entry for meditate session #1125

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat: BackwardBitReader.init completeness theorems (#1126)

Prove that `highBitPos` returns `some` for nonzero bytes, and that
`BackwardBitReader.init` succeeds when the input range is valid and
the last byte is nonzero. These are needed to show backward bitstream
initialization succeeds for well-formed Zstd compressed blocks.

commit.message:
Strip embedding/search_vector from all queries and add progressive disclosure result shaping

Search results were returning raw 768-dim vector embeddings and tsvector data,
bloating context windows. Fixed at three layers:

- DB layer: explicit column selections in SessionSearch, Observations, Summaries,
  Timeline, PgVectorSearchStrategy, StructuredSearchStrategy
- Strategy layer: stopped re-adding embedding:null/search_vector:null to results
- Route layer: progressive disclosure shaping per tier:
  - search: compact index (id, type, title, subtitle, project, concepts, score, epoch)
  - timeline: moderate (+ narrative, files_modified)
  - get_observations: full detail minus internals (no memory_session_id, content_hash,
    discovery_tokens, created_at)

Added 32 unit tests covering field containment, bloat prevention, and token budgets.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Fix backspace showing ^? by using char-by-char input reading

Replace Python's input() with custom _read_line() that reads one
character at a time and handles backspace/DEL explicitly. The
terminal's built-in line editor breaks when ANSI scroll regions
are used, causing ^? to appear instead of deleting characters.

Windows uses msvcrt.getwch(), Unix/WSL uses tty cbreak mode.
Also supports Ctrl+U (kill line), Ctrl+C, Ctrl+D, and silently
consumes arrow-key escape sequences.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

repository.name: SudoLabs
repository.description: Terminal-based cybersecurity hacking lab with AI-powered guidance, Docker targets, and HackTheBox support. Hunt vulnerable machines, get progressive hints from Claude, and track your progress through wolf-themed ranks.

commit.message:
Fix all 30 code review findings across 3 tiers

Tier 1 (Critical/High): SQL injection prevention via parameterized queries,
removed hardcoded API key, added auth to HTTP server, fixed unsafe force-unwrap,
added migration versioning, error propagation, and observer cleanup.

Tier 2 (Medium): SIGTERM handler for daemon, Content-Length body read loop,
TOCTOU race prevention in snapshots, query limits in ContextBuilder,
accessibility labels on all icon-only buttons and color indicators,
confirmation dialogs on destructive actions.

Tier 3 (Low): Cross-platform PlatformSocket abstraction replacing Darwin.send,
#if canImport guards for Security/CommonCrypto/CryptoKit, portable backup
directory, #if os(macOS) guard for launchctl, extracted runAsync helper
replacing 15 semaphore blocks, shared EmptyStateView, ViewerDefaults
constants, and ViewerColors semantic tokens.

75 tests pass, all 5 targets build clean.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
